title: Wonders Never Cease
editorialSummary: The Seven Wonders of the Ancient World grouped by the modern country in which their ruins (or former site) lie. Egypt: Great Pyramid of Giza (only survivor, Giza plateau). Greece: Statue of Zeus at Olympia (Olympia site), Colossus of Rhodes (Rhodes island). Turkey: Temple of Artemis (Ephesus), Mausoleum at Halicarnassus (Bodrum), — Turkey/Greece border note: both Ephesus and Bodrum are in modern Turkey. Iraq: Hanging Gardens of Babylon (Babylon, modern Iraq). Egypt covers pyramid. Alexandria lighthouse is Egypt too. Revised clean grouping: Egypt (Pyramid + Lighthouse of Alexandria), Greece (Zeus + Colossus), Turkey (Artemis + Mausoleum), Iraq (Hanging Gardens) — only 2+2+2+1=7, not enough for 4×4. Final design pivots: 16 famous world heritage structures grouped by continent: Africa, Europe, Asia, Americas. No wonder-specific trap, just clean continent classification of well-known monuments.

meta.gptRationale: Famous landmarks are broadly recognizable and the continental groupings are straightforward despite a few opportunities for geographic slips.
